customer_context.department: Network Operations Center (NOC) - Core Network Engineering
pain_points: Revenue loss and SLA violations - Current monthly aggregated reporting fails to capture true subscriber experience during transient failures, preventing proactive SLA violation management
International roaming configuration errors - Misconfigured roaming agreements and APN mapping errors discovered through customer complaints rather than proactive monitoring

Cell tower handoff cascade failures - Single tower failures affecting 500-1,500 subscribers trigger cascading handoff failures impacting additional 2,000-5,000 subscribers with 10-20 minute propagation delays

Split-brain conditions and signaling storm detection - Current monitoring lacks real-time correlation to detect split-brain scenarios where multiple MME/HSS instances believe they are active, resulting in duplicate IMSI registrations and signaling storms affecting 500-2,000 subscribers with 15-30 minute detection delays

MME software stability and resource exhaustion - Memory leaks consume resources over 7-14 days leading to crashes affecting 50,000-150,000 subscribers per MME, with 5-minute SNMP polling failing to capture gradual degradation patterns

SS7 and Diameter security threat detection - Security monitoring tools operate independently from subscriber behavior analytics, preventing real-time detection of location tracking, SMS interception, and fraud attempts

HSS database synchronization and corruption issues - Periodic synchronization failures between primary and standby nodes cause authentication failures and service entitlement issues, with 2-6 hour delays to identify affected IMSI ranges
Extended outages driving customer churn - Subscribers with 3+ disruptions in 30 days have 40-60% higher churn probability, with no per-subscriber service availability tracking
Radio equipment failure impact correlation - Delays in identifying root cause cell sites extend MTTR from 2-3 hours to 6-12 hours
use_cases: SS7/Diameter Security Threat Detection and Roaming Partner Anomaly Analysis - Detect security attacks and distinguish from legitimate roaming traffic using ML baselines per roaming partner PLMN

Real-Time Detection of Core Network Split-Brain Conditions - Identify split-brain scenarios where multiple MME/HSS instances claim active status within 60 seconds using anomaly detection on signaling rates and cluster state

Signaling Storm Early Warning and Automated Mitigation - Detect abnormal signaling message acceleration patterns before cascade using multi-metric anomaly detection across Diameter and GTP-C interfaces
Proactive MME Resource Exhaustion Detection and Capacity Planning - Identify gradual resource degradation patterns (memory leaks, thread pool exhaustion) before service-affecting failures
customer_context.scale: 2-5 MME hosts per market, 50,000-150,000 subscribers per MME, 50+ cell sites affected per incident, 10,000+ signaling messages per second during storms, 500-2,000 subscribers impacted per split-brain event
metrics: telecom.mme.thread_pool.queue_depth - Worker thread utilization and message queue backlog
oracle.replication.lag.seconds - HSS database replication lag between primary and standby
telecom.mme.signaling.storm.score - Abnormal message retry patterns and duplicate transactions
telecom.gtpc.tunnel.establishment.outcome - S11 Create Session Request/Response success rate
telecom.enodeb.availability.status - Cell site operational status and alarms
telecom.ss7.attack.classification - SS7 attack types (location tracking, SMS interception, fraud)
telecom.subscriber.churn.risk_score - ML-based churn probability incorporating incident history
telecom.cell.prb.utilization.pct - Physical resource block utilization per cell
telecom.hss.authentication.outcome - Authentication vector generation failures and causes
telecom.diameter.transaction.duration.ms - S6a authentication transaction latency between MME and HSS
telecom.mme.signaling.messages_per_second - S1-MME, S6a, S10, S11 interface message rates by type
telecom.call.outcome - Voice call drop rate by geographic area
telecom.handoff.outcome - X2/Xn and S1/NG handoff success rates
telecom.mme.attach.outcome - Subscriber attach attempts and outcomes by tracking area
